messages.content: Several risk write-ups may describe the same underlying risk. Pick the best exemplar (sharpest mechanism, best evidence) and fold any UNIQUE early-warning indicators from the others into it. If they are genuinely different risks, keep the strongest as exemplar anyway only when they truly overlap — otherwise return the highest-scored id with no folded indicators and note they are distinct.

content: {"keepId":"risk.boundary-substitution.large-operator-channel-forecloses-to-odm","rationale":"All eight write-ups describe the same underlying risk: the venture's declared first channel (large operators, 0.40 buyer share resting on a single misread company-level order-mix stat) is structurally foreclosed to a pre-launch entrant within the Year-1 window. The variants differ only in the named mechanism for foreclosure — global framework agreements, AVL machinery, NIS2 security certification, ODM/white-label rack integration, and qualification-cycle time — but all converge on the same model impact: the largest buyer cell should be excluded from reachable scope, not ramped slowly. The ODM/boundary-substitution write-up (judge 18, highest) has the sharpest mechanism: it explains WHY the demand relocates (AI-density racks procured as integrated units) rather than merely asserting the channel is slow, connecting the exclusion to a concrete industry migration into cust.oem where the venture has no channel plan. I fold in the genuinely distinct early-warning indicators from the others: the CRM/pipeline-routing signals (venture-side evidence), the security-certification prerequisite (NIS2 angle), and the internal qualification-funnel progress tracker — these observe different signals than the ODM/procurement-structure indicators already present.","foldedIndicators":[{"signal":"Where inbound and early pipeline demand actually routes — channel of the first 10 qualified conversations","where":"The venture's own CRM","threshold":">70% of engaged demand arrives via integrator/OEM/distributor rather than operator direct","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Security-certification prerequisites (ISO 27001, IEC 62443) in operator supplier-registration portals for connected facility equipment","where":"Equinix/NTT/Vantage/Data4 supplier onboarding portals; TED procurement notices for DC fit-outs","threshold":"ISO 27001 or IEC 62443 listed as mandatory for connected facility equipment","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Venture's own qualification-funnel progress with the first two target operators","where":"Internal CRM / operator procurement correspondence, reviewed monthly","threshold":"No pilot-pod agreement signed within 4 months of launch","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Distributor and rack-integrator line-card openings for third-party intelligent PDUs","where":"CE electrical distributor price lists (Rexel, Sonepar) and rack-integrator OEM programs","threshold":"≥1 CE distributor actively onboarding a new PDU brand","updates":"decreases"}]}
